mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, with Rank IC consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.5%, labor market slack suppresses wage growth and consumer spending expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 7.5%, or the signal yields a negative Rank IC over a 2-year rolling evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals synchronized consumer and builder confidence, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive movement of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window, or the interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, rapid month-over-month increases in the industrial production index reflect accelerating factory output and capacity utilization, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns specifically during post-shock recovery periods, with out-of-sample directional accuracy dropping below 52% over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly expansions in real gross domestic product confirm sustained economic growth, which supports corporate earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no significant predictive relationship with 60-day forward returns, with Rank IC remaining below 0.02 across multiple rolling 3-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 230.0, inflation expectations accelerate and force tighter monetary policy pricing, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 230.0, or the threshold signal yields a negative Rank IC over a 2-year rolling evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates indicates labor market tightening and wage pressure, which compresses corporate margins and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict negative 5-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample walk-forward testing over a 3-year horizon.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansion regimes, decelerating industrial production signals peak economic activity and impending earnings deterioration, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO deceleration fails to predict negative 60-day returns during late-cycle expansions, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 48% over a 3-year rolling window.
